question: Which country won the 2014 FIFA World Cup title in Brazil?
fact: Germany won their 4th title after defeating Argentina 1-0 in the final.

question: The 2014 World Cup final was held at the Maracanã Stadium in Rio de Janeiro.
fact: The Maracanã is one of only two stadiums to host two different World Cup finals (1950 and 2014).

question: Who won the Golden Boot as the top scorer of the 2014 World Cup?
fact: Colombia's James Rodríguez scored 6 goals, including the 'Goal of the Tournament' against Uruguay.

question: Who holds the record for most World Cup goals in history (achieved in 2014)?
fact: Klose scored his 16th goal during the 7-1 semi-final against Brazil, surpassing Ronaldo.

question: Brazil lost the 2014 semi-final to Germany by a score of 7-1.
fact: Known as the 'Mineirazo', it remains Brazil's largest-ever World Cup defeat.

question: Match the 2014 World Cup star to their national team:
fact: Tim Howard set a record in 2014 for most saves in a single World Cup match (15 vs Belgium).

question: Which country made its World Cup debut in the 2014 edition?
fact: They were the only nation making their first appearance that year.

question: Costa Rica reached the quarter-finals of the 2014 World Cup for the first time.
fact: Costa Rica topped a group featuring three former champions: England, Italy, and Uruguay.

question: What was the name of the official 2014 mascot (an armadillo)?
fact: The name Fuleco is a portmanteau of the Portuguese words for Football (Futebol) and Ecology (Ecologia).

question: Match the Winner to the year they won their FIRST title:
fact: Only 8 countries have ever won the World Cup.

question: Which team was the only one to remain undefeated in the 2010 World Cup?
fact: Even though they were eliminated in the group stage, they drew all three matches.

question: The FIFA World Cup takes place every four years.
fact: It has been held every 4 years since 1930, except during WWII (1942 and 1946).

question: Who was the first player to score a hat-trick in a World Cup Final?
fact: He scored three for England against West Germany in 1966.

question: Match the iconic stadium to its city:
fact: The Azteca and Maracanã are the only stadiums to host two finals.

question: How many teams participated in the 2014 FIFA World Cup?
fact: 2014 was the penultimate 32-team tournament before the expansion to 48.

question: The 'Jabulani' was the official match ball of the 2014 World Cup.
fact: The 2014 ball was the 'Brazuca'. The Jabulani was used in 2010.

question: Who is the only manager to win two FIFA World Cups?
fact: Pozzo coached Italy to victory in 1934 and 1938.

question: Match the country to its total World Cup wins (as of 2022):
fact: Brazil's last title came in 2002.

question: Which African nation was the first to reach a World Cup quarter-final?
fact: Cameroon broke the barrier in 1990 led by 38-year-old Roger Milla.

question: Pelé is the only player to have won three FIFA World Cups.
fact: He won in 1958, 1962, and 1970.
